varieties.name: Azadirachta indica (Standard Type)
varieties.origin: Indian subcontinent, widely planted Queensland since 1940s-1960s
varieties.yield_notes: 10-20kg fruit per mature tree annually, variable by provenance
varieties.growing_notes: Generally pest-resistant due to natural insecticidal compounds. May suffer winter dieback in severe cold spells.
varieties.availability: Widely available from Queensland nurseries, garden centers (Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, All Rare Herbs, Ross Creek Tropicals, local markets). Seeds and seedlings commonly sold.

varieties.name: Neem Grafted on White Cedar Rootstock
varieties.yield_notes: 15-25kg fruit per tree, more consistent than seedling trees
varieties.availability: Available from specialized Queensland nurseries (Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery grafted stock). Higher cost than seedling trees but superior cold adaptation.

varieties.name: North Indian Provenances (Subtropical Adapted)
varieties.origin: Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, Gujarat (northern India)
varieties.availability: Seeds occasionally available from Australian seed importers, specialty nurseries may source. Check with Queensland Department of Agriculture biosecurity requirements.

varieties.name: Australian Darwin/Northern Territory Selections
varieties.origin: Naturalized populations Darwin, Katherine River system (established 1960s-1980s)
varieties.availability: Not commercially available due to weed status in NT. Queensland nursery stock likely descended from these lines.

varieties.name: Queensland Heritage Trees (Historical Plantings)
varieties.origin: Mackay, Fraser Coast, Brisbane region trees planted 1940s-1980s
varieties.availability: Seeds occasionally available from mature trees on Fraser Coast and Mackay properties (check local tree farmers, Gumtree listings). Most Queensland nursery stock likely descended from these heritage lines.

varieties.name: Sweet Neem Type
varieties.availability: Not commercially distinguished in Australia. Sweet/bitter trait varies by individual tree genetics. May inquire with specialized Asian herb nurseries.

varieties.name: Bitter Neem Type
varieties.availability: Most Australian neem likely falls into 'bitter' category as original plantings were for insecticide production. Standard nursery stock.

varieties.name: High Azadirachtin Selections
varieties.availability: Not commercially available in Australia. Research material only. May become available if commercial neem industry develops in Queensland.

varieties.name: Aryaveepu (Standard Kerala Neem) 🏆
varieties.origin: Indigenous throughout Kerala, naturalized for centuries
varieties.yield_notes: 20-40kg fruit per tree, excellent productivity in high rainfall
varieties.growing_notes: Highly resistant to pests and diseases in Kerala climate. Natural insecticidal properties prevent most infestations.
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout Kerala - virtually every village has neem trees. Seeds and seedlings from Greens of Kerala, Kerala Forest Research Institute (Peechi), local nurseries, ayurvedic gardens, temple grounds. Often given freely by neighbors.

varieties.name: South Indian Provenances (Kerala-Type) 🏆
varieties.origin: Kerala, Tamil Nadu (Kanyakumari to coastal plains), Karnataka (Western Ghats)
varieties.availability: Seeds from mature trees throughout Kerala. Tamil Nadu Agricultural University (Coimbatore), Kerala Forest Research Institute (Peechi), Karnataka forest nurseries. Local collection from village trees.

varieties.name: Coastal Kerala Selections 🌱
varieties.origin: Coastal districts - Alappuzha, Ernakulam, Kollam, Thrissur
varieties.availability: Seeds from coastal area trees. Contact Alappuzha, Kollam district forest offices or coastal ayurvedic gardens.

varieties.name: Western Ghats Hill Selections 🌱
varieties.origin: Kerala hill regions - Idukki, Wayanad, Pathanamthitta districts (200-700m elevation)
varieties.availability: Kerala Forest Research Institute (Peechi), Idukki district forest nurseries, Wayanad spice plantation suppliers.

varieties.name: Andhra Pradesh Provenances
varieties.origin: Coastal Andhra Pradesh - Krishna, Guntur, Visakhapatnam districts
varieties.availability: Andhra Pradesh State Forest Department, IFGTB Coimbatore, sometimes available through Kerala seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Tamil Nadu Selections (TN Series)
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University (TNAU), IFGTB Coimbatore selections
varieties.availability: IFGTB Coimbatore (research material, limited public access), TNAU nurseries. May become available commercially if seed orchard established.

varieties.name: Karnataka Selections
varieties.origin: Karnataka dry forest areas and Western Ghats regions
varieties.availability: Karnataka State Forest Department, Western Ghats research stations. Specify high-rainfall source when ordering.

varieties.name: Bangladesh Provenance
varieties.origin: Bangladesh deltaic regions - similar monsoon climate
varieties.availability: Not readily available in India due to import restrictions. May be accessed through international neem network research programs.

varieties.name: Myanmar (Burma) Selections
varieties.origin: Central dry zone Burma, possibly original native range of species
varieties.availability: Not available in India. Academic interest only.

varieties.name: Thai Neem (Azadirachta indica var. siamensis)
varieties.availability: Not commercially available in India. Seeds would require plant quarantine approval. Academic/research interest. Thai neem represents interesting genetic resource for Kerala's high-rainfall conditions.

varieties.name: Veppampoo (Neem Flower) Selections 🏆
varieties.availability: Not formally distinguished. Ask for seeds from trees with abundant, mild-flavored flowers. Ayurvedic gardens, temple grounds may have preferred trees.

varieties.name: Ayurvedic Garden Selections 🏆
varieties.origin: Maintained in Kerala ayurvedic institutions, hospitals, and traditional vaidya gardens
varieties.availability: Seeds from ayurvedic institutions (Arya Vaidya Sala Kottakkal, Kerala Ayurveda Hospital), traditional medicine gardens. Often shared freely for planting. Greens of Kerala may supply.

varieties.name: Temple/Sacred Grove Trees 🏆
varieties.availability: Seeds may be collected with temple permission (respectfully, not during festivals). Many temples will share seeds gladly. Sacred grove conservation projects may supply. Contact Kerala Forest Research Institute for sacred grove genetic material.
